title: Day 2 — Chat Completions & compatible APIs
summary: HTTP vs SDK · Gemini · Ollama · DeepSeek via same OpenAI client shape.

sections.heading: Today's goal
sections.remember: **Chat Completions** = send a conversation, get the next assistant message — same idea for OpenAI, Gemini, and **Ollama**.

sections.heading: HTTP vs Python SDK
sections.remember: `POST .../v1/chat/completions` + Bearer token = same as `openai.chat.completions.create` — the library is not the model.
points: SDK is convenience; understand HTTP for debugging.

sections.heading: OpenAI-compatible APIs
sections.remember: Change **`base_url`** + **`api_key`** → keep `chat.completions.create` — **one code shape, many vendors**.

sections.heading: Google Gemini
sections.remember: `GOOGLE_API_KEY` + Gemini OpenAI-compatible base URL — same messages list.
points: Lab checks key format (e.g. starts with expected prefix).

sections.heading: Ollama local
points: Data stays on your machine — homework: rebuild Day 1 summarizer with Ollama.
Avoid huge local models if RAM is limited.

sections.heading: DeepSeek via Ollama
sections.remember: Reasoning-style models (e.g. **deepseek-r1**) can run locally through Ollama — same API pattern.
points: When to use: zero API cost, privacy, prototyping.
When to use cloud: best quality, speed, no local GPU/RAM fight.

cheatSheet: Chat Completions = messages in, assistant out
